gloss.en: a little more or less than

words.example: It costs about $10.

gloss.en: the process of doing something to make something happen

words.example: The time has come for action.

gloss.en: a thing you do for interest or pleasure

words.example: Swimming is my favorite activity.

gloss.en: a person who performs in films or plays

words.example: He is a famous movie actor.

gloss.en: to put something with something else to increase it

words.example: Next add the flour.

gloss.en: a flying vehicle with wings and engines

words.example: The aeroplane landed safely.

gloss.en: feeling fear; frightened

words.example: Don't be afraid.

gloss.en: the time from noon until about six o'clock

words.example: I will see you this afternoon.

gloss.en: one more time; on another occasion

words.example: This must never happen again.

gloss.en: in the past, before now

words.example: She left two weeks ago.

gloss.en: to have the same opinion as somebody

words.example: When he said that, I had to agree.

gloss.en: a flying vehicle with wings and engines

words.example: We flew home by airplane.

gloss.en: a place where planes land and take off

words.example: We met her at the airport.

gloss.en: in addition; too

words.example: She also speaks a little Italian.

gloss.en: at all times; on every occasion

words.example: He is always late for work.

gloss.en: the form of 'be' used with 'I'

words.example: I am very happy today.

gloss.en: one; used before a vowel sound

words.example: She ate an apple.

gloss.en: also; in addition to

words.example: I like bread and butter.

gloss.en: having strong bad feelings about something

words.example: Her behaviour really made me angry.

gloss.en: a living creature that is not a plant or human

words.example: A dog is a friendly animal.

gloss.en: one more; an extra thing or person

words.example: Would you like another drink?

gloss.en: something you say or write to react to a question

words.example: I knew the answer to the question.

gloss.en: any person at all

words.example: Is anyone there?

gloss.en: any thing at all

words.example: Would you like anything else?

gloss.en: a round fruit with red or green skin

words.example: She ate a red apple.

gloss.en: the fourth month of the year

words.example: She was born in April.

gloss.en: clothing worn to keep your clothes clean while cooking

words.example: The cook wore a white apron.

gloss.en: the form of 'be' used with you/we/they

words.example: They are my best friends.

gloss.en: to get to a place

words.example: I'll wait until they arrive.

gloss.en: painting, drawing or sculpture made by people

words.example: She loves modern art.

gloss.en: a piece of writing in a newspaper or magazine

words.example: I read an interesting article today.

gloss.en: to say a question to get information

words.example: Can I ask you a question?

gloss.en: used to say where something is or happens

words.example: We met at the corner of the street.

gloss.en: the eighth month of the year

words.example: She was born in August.

gloss.en: the sister of your father or mother

words.example: My aunt lives near us.

gloss.en: the season between summer and winter

words.example: The leaves fall in autumn.

gloss.en: not asleep

words.example: The baby is still awake.

gloss.en: at a distance from a place

words.example: The beach is a mile away.

gloss.en: a very young child

words.example: The baby is crying.

gloss.en: unpleasant; not good

words.example: I have some bad news for you.

gloss.en: a container used to carry things

words.example: He was carrying a heavy bag.

gloss.en: a round object used in games and sports

words.example: The boy kicked the ball.

gloss.en: a long curved yellow fruit

words.example: I eat a banana every morning.

gloss.en: a small group of musicians who play together

words.example: They are in a rock band.

gloss.en: a place that keeps or lends money

words.example: I keep my money in the bank.

gloss.en: a container you fill with water to wash in

words.example: I take a bath every night.

gloss.en: a room with a bath and often a toilet

words.example: Go and wash your hands in the bathroom.

gloss.en: to exist or have a quality

words.example: I want to be a doctor.

gloss.en: an area of sand next to the sea

words.example: We played on the beach all day.

gloss.en: very pleasing to look at

words.example: She is a beautiful woman.

gloss.en: for the reason that

words.example: I did it because he told me to.

gloss.en: to start to be something

words.example: She wants to become a teacher.

gloss.en: a piece of furniture for sleeping on

words.example: I go to bed at ten.

gloss.en: a room for sleeping in

words.example: My bedroom is upstairs.

gloss.en: to start doing something

words.example: Let's begin the lesson now.

gloss.en: at the back of something

words.example: The dog ran behind the car.

gloss.en: to feel certain something is true

words.example: I don't believe you!

gloss.en: at a lower level or place

words.example: They live on the floor below.

gloss.en: a vehicle with two wheels that you ride

words.example: He rode his bicycle to school.

gloss.en: large in size or amount

words.example: They live in a big house.

gloss.en: a bicycle

words.example: I ride my bike every day.

gloss.en: an animal with feathers and wings that can fly

words.example: A small bird sat on the tree.

gloss.en: the yearly date when you were born

words.example: Happy Birthday to you!

gloss.en: having the darkest colour, like night

words.example: He drives a shiny black car.

gloss.en: having the colour of a clear sky

words.example: She has bright blue eyes.

gloss.en: a small vehicle that travels on water

words.example: We crossed the lake by boat.

gloss.en: the whole physical form of a person or animal

words.example: The human body is amazing.

gloss.en: a shop that sells books

words.example: I bought a novel at the bookstore.

gloss.en: not interesting

words.example: The film was really boring.

gloss.en: to come into life

words.example: I was born in 1976.

gloss.en: the two; one as well as the other

words.example: Both women were French.

gloss.en: a container with a narrow neck for liquids

words.example: She drank a bottle of water.

gloss.en: a container with stiff sides for holding things

words.example: We packed our things in a box.

gloss.en: a male child or young male person

words.example: The little boy is playing.

gloss.en: a man someone has a romantic relationship with

words.example: She's got a new boyfriend.

gloss.en: a baked food made from flour and water

words.example: I bought a loaf of bread.

gloss.en: the first meal of the day

words.example: We were having breakfast when I arrived.

gloss.en: to come to a place with something

words.example: Don't forget to bring your books.

gloss.en: a boy or man with the same parents as you

words.example: My brother is older than me.

gloss.en: having the colour of earth or coffee

words.example: She has brown eyes.

gloss.en: a round open container with a handle

words.example: He filled the bucket with water.

gloss.en: to make something by putting parts together

words.example: They want to build a new house.

gloss.en: a structure with a roof and walls

words.example: That is a tall building.

gloss.en: a round flat cake of meat in a bun

words.example: I had a burger for lunch.

gloss.en: a large road vehicle that carries many passengers

words.example: I go to work by bus.

gloss.en: the activity of buying and selling for money

words.example: He runs a small business.

gloss.en: having a lot to do

words.example: Are you busy tonight?

gloss.en: a soft yellow food made from cream

words.example: She spread butter on the bread.

gloss.en: an insect with large colourful wings

words.example: A butterfly landed on the flower.

gloss.en: to get something by paying money

words.example: Where did you buy that dress?

gloss.en: goodbye

words.example: Bye! See you next week.

gloss.en: a place that sells drinks and simple meals

words.example: We had coffee at a small cafe.

gloss.en: a sweet baked food made from flour and sugar

words.example: She ate a slice of cake.

gloss.en: the act of speaking to someone on the phone

words.example: I got a call from my mother.

gloss.en: a device for taking photographs

words.example: She bought a new camera.

gloss.en: a sweet food made from sugar

words.example: The child loves to eat candy.

gloss.en: the negative of can; cannot do something

words.example: I cannot believe the price!

gloss.en: a road vehicle with an engine and four wheels

words.example: She got into the car and drove off.

gloss.en: a piece of stiff paper for writing on

words.example: I sent her a birthday card.

gloss.en: to hold and take something from place to place

words.example: He was carrying a large bag.

gloss.en: a small furry animal kept as a pet

words.example: The cat is sleeping on the bed.

gloss.en: a small disc that stores music or data

words.example: His new album is on CD.

gloss.en: the result of something becoming different

words.example: We're hoping for a change in the weather.

gloss.en: a food made from milk, often yellow or white

words.example: I like cheese on my sandwich.

gloss.en: a bird kept for its eggs and meat

words.example: We had chicken for dinner.

gloss.en: a young human who is not yet an adult

words.example: She is a clever child.

gloss.en: a sweet brown food made from cocoa

words.example: He gave me a bar of chocolate.

gloss.en: to decide which thing you want

words.example: You choose—I can't decide.

gloss.en: a building where films are shown

words.example: We went to the cinema last night.

gloss.en: a large and important town

words.example: She lives in a big city.

gloss.en: a group of students taught together

words.example: We were in the same class at school.

gloss.en: a person in the same class as you

words.example: She is my classmate at school.

gloss.en: a room where students are taught

words.example: The teacher is in the classroom.

gloss.en: not dirty

words.example: Are your hands clean?

gloss.en: a device that shows the time on a wall

words.example: The clock on the wall says noon.

gloss.en: things you wear, like shirts and trousers

words.example: I bought some new clothes.

gloss.en: having many clouds in the sky

words.example: The sky is grey and cloudy today.

gloss.en: a group for people who share an interest

words.example: She joined the tennis club.

gloss.en: a piece of clothing worn over others for warmth

words.example: Put on your coat, it's cold.

gloss.en: a hot drink made from roasted beans

words.example: I drink coffee every morning.
